quick_facts.label: Recruiting stream
quick_facts.value: Trains Clerk is an undergraduate Non-Technical Popular Categories (NTPC) post recruited by Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) through a Centralised Employment Notice (CEN).

quick_facts.label: Education requirement
quick_facts.value: In CEN 07/2025, the prescribed qualification for Trains Clerk was 12th (+2 stage) or equivalent with at least 50% aggregate marks; the notice exempted SC/ST, PwBD, ex-servicemen and candidates with qualifications higher than 12th from the 50% requirement.

quick_facts.label: Selection route
quick_facts.value: The CEN 07/2025 process specified first- and second-stage computer-based tests (CBTs), followed by document verification and a Railway medical examination. Unlike the two typist posts in that notice, Trains Clerk had no typing-skill test requirement.

quick_facts.label: Medical standard
quick_facts.value: The post carried medical standard A-3 in CEN 07/2025. The notice specified vision requirements including 6/9 distant vision in each eye (with or without glasses, lens power not exceeding 2D) and passing colour, binocular, night and myopic-vision tests; it also stated that candidates who had LASIK or other refractive-error corrective surgery were not eligible for A-3 posts in that CEN.

quick_facts.label: Pay context
quick_facts.value: CEN 07/2025 listed Trains Clerk at Level 2 of the 7th CPC pay matrix with initial basic pay of ₹19,900. This is dated recruitment-notice context, not a guarantee of current in-hand pay, which varies with admissible allowances and deductions.

quick_facts.label: Recent vacancy context
quick_facts.value: CEN 07/2025 listed 77 Trains Clerk vacancies across all RRBs. Vacancy counts are recruitment-cycle-specific and should not be treated as an ongoing number of openings.

sections.heading: What a Trains Clerk does
paragraphs: A Trains Clerk supports railway operating work by maintaining train and stock-related records. A Northern Railway operating-department work-study report describes train-passing duties such as noting coach numbers of inbound and outbound trains, recording train movements and coach attach/detach details, and maintaining coaching-stock records.

The same report identifies assignments that can include entering summaries for PRS and NTES through the Coaching Operations Information System (COIS), updating train arrival, departure and coach-composition displays, maintaining goods-yard records, and preparing the composition of originating trains for the charting section. Actual duty allocation depends on the station, terminal, yard, traffic volume and local operating arrangement.
bullets: Record movements, coach numbers and attach/detach changes for inbound and outbound trains.
Maintain coaching-stock and, where assigned, goods-yard records.
Update operational and passenger-information data such as arrivals, departures and coach composition.
Prepare or communicate train-composition information for charting and operating staff.

sections.heading: How to enter through RRB NTPC
paragraphs: The direct external route is to apply when an RRB publishes an undergraduate NTPC CEN that includes Trains Clerk vacancies. The 2025 CEN required online application and treated the post as a Level-2 undergraduate post; its eligibility date, age limits, vacancies, reservations, application dates and relaxations applied only to that recruitment cycle.

For Trains Clerk in CEN 07/2025, candidates progressed through CBT-1 and CBT-2, with normalized CBT-2 marks used for shortlisting to document verification for posts without a typing test. Candidates called for document verification then had to pass the medical fitness examination conducted by the Railway Administration. Appointment is consequently contingent on eligibility verification and medical fitness, not examination marks alone.
bullets: Meet the specific CEN's nationality, education, age and category requirements.
Apply online to one chosen RRB during the notified application window.
Clear CBT-1 and CBT-2 under the applicable RRB NTPC scheme.
Complete document verification and meet the A-3 medical standard.

sections.heading: Skills that fit the role
paragraphs: The documented duties point to a role where accuracy, concentration and orderly record-keeping are central: the clerk must capture train, coach, attachment/detachment and stock information correctly. Because duties can include COIS entries, PRS/NTES summaries and display updates, practical computer-data-entry confidence and careful checking of operational information are useful.

The role also calls for clear handover and coordination habits. The work-study report includes recording and delivering coaching-traffic messages and sending composition information to charting, so candidates should be prepared to work with operating, station and charting colleagues under time-sensitive conditions. These skill implications are derived from the listed duties rather than a separate RRB competency standard.
bullets: Accurate data entry, reconciliation and record maintenance.
Attention to train numbers, coach composition and operational changes.
Comfort using operational information systems and digital displays.
Clear communication, shift handover discipline and coordination with railway operating teams.

sections.heading: Work setting and hours
paragraphs: The post sits within Indian Railways' operating environment. Indian Railways' operating manual places Trains Clerks alongside Head Trains Clerks and Chief Trains Clerks in the divisional control organization, whose train-operation monitoring functions run round the clock.

A Northern Railway work-study report describes several Trains Clerk functions being staffed in eight-hour shifts, including train-passing, COIS and display work. This supports expecting shift-based work at busy stations, terminals, yards or operational offices, although the precise roster, location and mix of duties vary by railway zone and posting.
bullets: Potential settings include stations, terminals, yards, train branches and divisional operating/control offices.
Round-the-clock railway operations can require rostered shifts.
Work combines desk-based records and system updates with coordination around live train operations.

sections.heading: Career context and progression
paragraphs: Indian Railways operating documents show a Trains Clerk hierarchy that includes Trains Clerk, Head Trains Clerk and Chief Trains Clerk. South Central Railway likewise lists TNC, Senior TNC and CTNC among Operating Department categories. These sources establish the existence of more senior trains-clerk designations, but they do not establish an automatic promotion pathway for every employee.

Progression is governed by the applicable railway establishment rules, seniority, suitability/selection arrangements, vacancies and any restructuring instructions in force in the employee's railway or unit. Candidates should therefore consult their appointing railway's current personnel circulars rather than assume a fixed timetable or guaranteed next post.
bullets: The operating hierarchy includes Trains Clerk, Head/Senior Trains Clerk and Chief Trains Clerk designations.
Promotion mechanics and timing are railway- and vacancy-dependent.
Internal rules and current personnel notices are the authoritative source for an individual employee's progression options.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: For the CEN 07/2025 recruitment cycle, Trains Clerk was advertised in 7th CPC Pay Level 2 with an initial basic pay of ₹19,900. Basic pay should not be confused with take-home pay: the latter depends on allowances, posting and deductions applicable at the time of appointment and can change with government orders.

The CEN's pay figure is useful for comparing the post with other notified NTPC positions, but candidates should use the specific CEN and the eventual appointment terms for the applicable compensation details.
bullets: Dated notice context: Pay Level 2, initial basic pay ₹19,900 in CEN 07/2025.
Allowances and deductions make actual monthly take-home pay variable.
Always verify pay and service terms in the applicable recruitment notice and appointment order.
